name: Element 115 Moscovium
description: Moscovium (Mc), also known as Element 115, is a synthetic superheavy element. Within the scientific community, Moscovium's existence is confirmed; it was first synthesized in 2003 by a joint team of Russian and American scientists at the Joint Institute for Nuclear Research (JINR) in Dubna, Russia. Its discovery was officially recognized in 2015, and it was named Moscovium in 2016 after the Moscow Oblast, where JINR is located. Moscovium is extremely radioactive and decays rapidly. Its isotopes have half-lives ranging from milliseconds to fractions of a second. Due to its instability, only a few atoms of Moscovium have ever been created, limiting its study. Some fringe theories, particularly within UFO conspiracy circles, claim that Element 115 possesses unusual properties, potentially including anti-gravity effects, stemming from alleged claims made by Bob Lazar in the late 1980s. Lazar claimed that Element 115, which he called 'Ununpentium' (its temporary name before official naming), could power alien spacecraft. Mainstream science refutes these claims, emphasizing that Moscovium is a highly unstable element with no known anti-gravity properties and that Lazar's claims lack scientific evidence and are widely considered pseudoscientific.
subtitle: Synthetic superheavy element; conspiracy claims.

claims.evidence: Bob Lazar's claims and some fringe websites suggest stability.
claims.criticism: Moscovium is an extremely unstable, synthetic element with very short half-lives.

claims.evidence: Bob Lazar claimed it could warp spacetime for propulsion.
claims.criticism: There is no scientific evidence to support anti-gravity properties for Moscovium or any element.

claims.claim: Element 115 is used in alien spacecraft technology.
claims.criticism: This claim is based on unsubstantiated testimony and lacks empirical evidence. It is considered pseudoscience.

keyFigures.name: Joint Institute for Nuclear Research (JINR)
keyFigures.role: Synthesized and confirmed the existence of Moscovium.
